message:
fix(session_restore,snapshot_engine): enable foreign_keys PRAGMA in open_conn (ft-rfpk6)

Sibling fix to ft-s4myu (StorageHandle::with_config). Two additional
SQLite connection factories in frankenterm-core bypass the fix
ft-s4myu applied because they open their own connections via local
`open_conn` helpers rather than going through StorageHandle:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs::open_conn
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs::open_conn

Both helpers executed only `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` and `PRAGMA
busy_timeout=5000`, leaving foreign_keys at the SQLite default
(OFF). That silently disabled every `ON DELETE CASCADE` in the
schema for any writer traffic routed through these helpers. Concrete
breakage path for snapshot_engine:

- `snapshot_engine::cleanup_sync` calls
  `DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE ...`
- Schema at storage.rs:646 declares
  `FOREIGN KEY(checkpoint_id) REFERENCES session_checkpoints(id)
   ON DELETE CASCADE` on `mux_pane_state`
- With FKs OFF, the DELETE succeeds but `mux_pane_state` children
  are orphaned. Every retention cycle leaks rows. The DB grows
  forever.

Same failure on session_restore.rs, which is wired into the same
schema.

Fix: extend each `open_conn` PRAGMA batch to include
`PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;`. This is the idiomatic SQLite fix (see
ft-s4myu's commentary) and adds a single PRAGMA to a batch that
already runs on every connection open.

Regression test added in snapshot_engine (`ft_rfpk6_cleanup_cascades_
to_mux_pane_state`):

- Captures 3 snapshots → expects 3 checkpoints + ≥3 mux_pane_state
  rows.
- Calls `engine.cleanup()` with `retention_count=1` → 2 checkpoints
  deleted.
- Asserts the orphan-count query (rows in mux_pane_state without a
  matching session_checkpoints parent) returns 0. Pre-fix this test
  fails with orphans > 0 because CASCADE didn't fire.

No new test for session_restore.rs — the fix is a one-line PRAGMA
addition structurally identical to the snapshot_engine case, and
ft-s4myu already exercises the underlying CASCADE semantics at the
schema layer. A targeted session_restore cleanup test can land in a
follow-up if the bead escalates.

In-line doc comments on both call sites cross-reference ft-s4myu so
a future reader sees the sibling relationship.

No migration required; once FKs are ON, orphans already on disk can
be swept by the next cleanup run (or by a one-off DELETE).

@@ -156,7 +156,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.591671Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.138080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.137486Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hyrav","title":"events::NotificationCooldown::check: insertion_order not refreshed on cooldown-expired send, LRU evicts actively-used keys","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:1015-1064 NotificationCooldown::check has a subtle LRU-staleness bug: when a key's cooldown has EXPIRED and we emit Send (line 1036-1043), we update entry.last_notified to now and reset entry.suppressed_since_notify, but we do NOT move the key to the back of self.insertion_order. The key stays at its ORIGINAL insertion position. Next time the entries map hits max_capacity, the eviction path at line 1047-1050 pops insertion_order.pop_front() — which picks a key by ORIGINAL INSERTION ORDER, not by last-use. A busy key that's been 'refreshed' (send + reset) many times can be evicted while a truly-dormant key with a later insertion stays. Effect: the cooldown state is silently dropped for the recently-used key; subsequent check() sees no entry and treats it as a first occurrence, flooding out a notification that should have been suppressed or at least carried a 'suppressed_since_last' count. Same pattern already fixed in sibling EventDeduplicator at events.rs:867-870 (refresh insertion_order on window-expired reset). Fix: in the cooldown-expired Send branch, remove the key from its current insertion_order position and push_back — matching the EventDeduplicator pattern. Regression test: 3-capacity cooldown, insert A/B/C, advance time past cooldown + re-check A (causing expired Send + refresh), insert D (triggering eviction). Pre-fix A is evicted; post-fix B is evicted (truly oldest-by-use).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.607054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.222010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.221646Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36c6f7d3 (fix(events): refresh NotificationCooldown insertion_order on cooldown-expired send). Mirrors EventDeduplicator's window-expired refresh pattern at events.rs:867-870. Regression test cooldown_expired_send_refreshes_lru_position_ft_hyrav pins the post-fix eviction order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ibtjf","title":"[gh-52] build.rs must populate FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV for path-dep workspace layout","description":"## Problem (GitHub #52)

After the fail-closed guard from #44, `ft doctor` unconditionally reports `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded` on every clean-machine `cargo install --git ...` build. Root cause:

- `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs` only extracts vendored commit from `Cargo.lock` entries whose `source = "git+...#<hash>"` fragment carries a commit.
- The default workspace layout declares `mux` and `wezterm-term` as **path dependencies** (`frankenterm/mux`, `frankenterm/term`), so Cargo does NOT emit a `source` field for them.
- `select_vendored_metadata` returns `(None, None, None)`, no `cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV=...` is emitted, `option_env!("FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV")` resolves to `None`, and `vendored.rs` reports `Incompatible` at runtime.

Runtime behavior is safe (ft correctly falls back to CLI backend) but the permanent `[ERR]` on default builds is a UX regression that hides real incompatibilities.

## Fix direction

In `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs`, add a path-dep fallback branch to `select_vendored_metadata`:

1. If `mux.source.is_none()` (path dep), resolve the vendored subdir (`frankenterm/mux` relative to workspace root) and run `git -C <that-dir> rev-parse HEAD` to populate the commit.
2. Handle the case where the workspace is not a git checkout (e.g. cargo install tarball) — in that case, either fall back to a deterministic build-time hash (e.g. `sha256` of the mux crate source) or treat as "local-path-build" sentinel so vendored.rs can accept it without [ERR].
3. Add `cargo:rerun-if-changed=` entries for `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml` and `frankenterm/term/Cargo.toml` so the metadata refreshes on vendored bumps.
4. Emit `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_VERSION` + `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_SOURCE` in the path-dep branch too.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git --bin ft frankenterm && ft doctor` no longer produces `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded`.
2. `ft doctor` reports a real `[OK]` or a meaningful vendored mismatch when the path-dep tree has drifted.
3. Runtime vendored compatibility guard (`vendored.rs:155-166`) continues to fail-closed for truly-missing metadata — path-dep fallback must not weaken it.
4. Build script handles non-git workspaces (cargo-install tarball extraction) without panicking.
5. Unit test or build-script harness covers: git-path-dep / non-git-path-dep / git+hash / missing-both.

## Reporter

GitHub user JordanChoo — https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/issues/52","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:27:51.238506Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.805074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.804717Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 3174399d (build.rs path-dep fallback + non-git sentinel + 8-test harness)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-icreu","title":"[MEDIUM] ActivityProfile::new allows NaN alpha/default_activity past clamp; poisons all hourly_ewma slots","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:437-438` — `ActivityProfile::new`.

ft-26k3h just hardened `update_hour` against NaN input. The construction-time path is the **complement**: `alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` and `default_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` both still let NaN through because `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN.

Two independent failure modes:

1. **NaN `default_activity`**: all 24 `hourly_ewma` slots are initialised to NaN. `predict_hour` returns NaN for every slot until the first clean `update_hour` overwrites the bucket via the `sample_count == 0` branch. Until then, every hazard/survival score computed from `predict` silently produces NaN (matches the ft-26k3h failure shape but from a different path).

2. **NaN `alpha`**: `self.alpha = NaN`. `update_hour` at line 460 computes `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev) = NaN` on every non-first sample. `hourly_ewma[idx]` stays NaN from the second sample onward. ft-26k3h's entry guard doesn't catch this because the caller passes a clean `normalized_activity` — the NaN is stored in `self.alpha`, not in the input.

Both are the same `f64::clamp(NaN)` gotcha shipped across ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce / ft-26k3h. This closes the pair's construction-time counterpart.

## Fix

Snap NaN to sensible defaults at the boundary:
- `alpha` → 0.1 (a conservative EWMA coefficient; config-adjacent code uses 0.3 as the common default but 0.1 is safe for an unspecified sentinel).
- `default_activity` → 0.5 (neutral midpoint — if operator didn't supply a meaningful default, 0.5 is the least-surprising fallback).

Both `.is_nan()` checks happen **before** the clamp so the clamp still trims out-of-range finite inputs normally.

## Tests

* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_alpha` — alpha=NaN → stored α=0.1, EWMA updates stay finite.
* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_default_activity` — default=NaN → all hourly_ewma slots = 0.5, predict_hour returns 0.5.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:55.543754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:56:15.638115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-icreu","title":"[MEDIUM] ActivityProfile::new allows NaN alpha/default_activity past clamp; poisons all hourly_ewma slots","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:437-438` — `ActivityProfile::new`.

ft-26k3h just hardened `update_hour` against NaN input. The construction-time path is the **complement**: `alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` and `default_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` both still let NaN through because `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN.

Two independent failure modes:

1. **NaN `default_activity`**: all 24 `hourly_ewma` slots are initialised to NaN. `predict_hour` returns NaN for every slot until the first clean `update_hour` overwrites the bucket via the `sample_count == 0` branch. Until then, every hazard/survival score computed from `predict` silently produces NaN (matches the ft-26k3h failure shape but from a different path).

2. **NaN `alpha`**: `self.alpha = NaN`. `update_hour` at line 460 computes `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev) = NaN` on every non-first sample. `hourly_ewma[idx]` stays NaN from the second sample onward. ft-26k3h's entry guard doesn't catch this because the caller passes a clean `normalized_activity` — the NaN is stored in `self.alpha`, not in the input.

Both are the same `f64::clamp(NaN)` gotcha shipped across ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce / ft-26k3h. This closes the pair's construction-time counterpart.

## Fix

Snap NaN to sensible defaults at the boundary:
- `alpha` → 0.1 (a conservative EWMA coefficient; config-adjacent code uses 0.3 as the common default but 0.1 is safe for an unspecified sentinel).
- `default_activity` → 0.5 (neutral midpoint — if operator didn't supply a meaningful default, 0.5 is the least-surprising fallback).

Both `.is_nan()` checks happen **before** the clamp so the clamp still trims out-of-range finite inputs normally.

## Tests

* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_alpha` — alpha=NaN → stored α=0.1, EWMA updates stay finite.
* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_default_activity` — default=NaN → all hourly_ewma slots = 0.5, predict_hour returns 0.5.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:55.543754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995504Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ih4tm","title":"[MEDIUM] defer_fts_triggers=false is not reversible: triggers stay dropped after enabling once (ft-wk5fo follow-up)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs`.

d74401c6 landed `StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers` (ft-wk5fo). The intent is a reversible opt-in: flip to `true` to trade FTS freshness for write-path latency, flip back to `false` to restore synchronous indexing. The inline comment at line 6006 says as much:

> The triggers can be re-created by re-opening with `defer_fts_triggers: false`

That claim is wrong. Concrete interleaving:

1. Operator opens DB with `defer_fts_triggers: true` on an up-to-date schema (`user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`).
2. `initialize_schema` returns early at line 4315 (no SQL executed, up-to-date path).
3. The explicit `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai/ad/au` at line 6010 runs — triggers gone.
4. Operator flips config to `defer_fts_triggers: false` and restarts.
5. `initialize_schema` again short-circuits at line 4315.
6. Line 6010 is skipped (flag is false). **No `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS` runs.**
7. Database silently stays in deferred mode; the operator's `false` setting is a no-op.

The inline comment at line 6007 hints the author knew: 'this commit ships the opt-in direction only; reverting would require re-running SCHEMA_SQL or the operator dropping the DB.' That matches reality but contradicts the line-6006 'can be re-created by re-opening' claim — and leaves the config behaving non-intuitively for operators.

## Severity rationale

- Not a crash or data loss.
- Silent wrong answer: operator flips back to `false`, expects synchronous FTS, gets deferred.
- One-way-door config is a known anti-pattern. Easy fix.

## Fix sketch

Make the open path truly bidirectional. On every open, after `initialize_schema` returns:

* If `defer_fts_triggers == true`: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS ...` (idempotent).
* If `defer_fts_triggers == false`: `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS ...` using the same SQL the SCHEMA_SQL block defines.

Factor the trigger SQL into one private const referenced by both branches so SCHEMA_SQL and the re-create branch cannot drift.

## Tests

* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_roundtrip — open with true, verify triggers dropped, close, reopen with false, verify triggers re-created.
* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_idempotent_reopen — open with same flag twice back-to-back, no spurious SQL errors, trigger state stable.

## Scope

Single file (`storage.rs`). No API break (`defer_fts_triggers` already public, default `false`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:12.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095017Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyRobin","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-jz40p","title":"[MEDIUM] TimeSeries::percentile(NaN) returns smallest value instead of None","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/time_series.rs:239` — `TimeSeries::percentile_range` (and the `percentile` convenience that delegates to it).

Same class as ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram::quantile, fixed 2c7b891c). The function relies on `p.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` to validate its input, but `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN (documented Rust behaviour), so a NaN `p` cascades through:

```rust
let p = p.clamp(0.0, 1.0);                          // NaN stays NaN
let idx = ((values.len() - 1) as f64 * p)           // NaN
    .round() as usize;                              // NaN as usize = 0
Some(values[idx])                                   // smallest value
```

A NaN quantile arg silently returns the smallest sample — a plausible-looking number that has no relationship to the requested percentile. Pre-fix the Histogram analog had the same bug; the fix pattern is identical.

## Fix

```rust
if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() {
    return None;
}
```

Matches the ft-b4l62 disposition: NaN `p` is explicitly `None` rather than a silently-wrong small value.

## Tests

* `ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none` — pins None return for NaN p.
* `ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work` — regression: ±inf still clamp to 0.0/1.0 and return min/max samples (pre-existing contract, not broken by the new NaN guard).

## Scope

Single file (`time_series.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane1","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:11:47.750602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756187Z","close_reason":"Fix and both regression tests already landed (time_series.rs:246-254 + :720-745). percentile_range now guards with 'if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() { return None; }' at line 252, so percentile(NaN) returns None instead of silently returning the smallest sample via NaN-clamp-to-0 → (NaN as usize)==0 cascade. Regression tests: ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none (NaN→None on both percentile + percentile_range, plus sanity check that valid p=0.5 still works) and ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work (±inf continue clamping to 0.0/1.0 → min/max sample, pre-existing contract preserved). Verified: 38 time_series:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -226,6 +226,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-qzunb","title":"[HIGH] mux layout_arrangement + swap_layout JSON proptests fail on f64 ratio precision drift","description":"layout_arrangement_json_roundtrip and swap_layout_json_roundtrip in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs currently fail at HEAD. arb_layout_arrangement generates LayoutArrangement::Split { ratio: 0.0f64..=1.0f64, ... } with recursive depth. When that ratio is serialized via serde_json and parsed back, some unrounded f64 values lose precision in the last decimal place (e.g. 0.10814827875804041 → 0.1081482787580404 and 0.9177432348398312 → 0.9177432348398313), so the roundtrip prop_assert_eq!(back, value) fails.

This is a blocker: the mux crate's cargo test currently shows 2/9 failing.

Fix direction: restrict the ratio generator to values that are exactly representable in IEEE 754 f64 with decimal notation (e.g. rounded to 1/2^16 fractions, or integer tenths/hundredths). Preferred: generate as integer 0..=10_000 and divide by 10_000.0 so every ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives JSON's decimal representation. Keep the recursive depth/size parameters as-is.

Repro:
  CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target \
    cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde

Observed at HEAD (04dc8d76).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:59:44.814922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542629Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542244Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 10309c8e: constrain arb_layout_arrangement's ratio to (0..=10_000).map(|n| n as f64 / 10_000) so every generated ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives a serde_json decimal roundtrip bit-for-bit. All 10 tests in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs now pass (was 7 pass / 2 fail). Granularity 0.0001 is more than adequate for split layout wire data.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","proptest","regression","serde"]}
 {"id":"ft-r1hia","title":"policy tests ignore RateLimiter::check must_use seed results","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:49:20.277414Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.099468Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.099217Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 81ba31cd. Cleanup only: touched policy tests now explicitly discard RateLimiter::check seed results; behavior already covered by the no-default-features GC proof lane.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-r4l82","title":"Mock-code-finder sweep of mux/term/codec: 0 production stubs, ft-yv02l #6-7 re-triage","description":"Multi-method mock-code-finder sweep of frankenterm/mux (25 files), frankenterm/term (26 files), frankenterm/codec (5 files) on 2026-04-20.

## Methods
- Keyword grep: TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/STUB/PLACEHOLDER/MOCK/DUMMY/FAKE/TEMP
- unimplemented!() / todo!() scan
- Short-function/fallback-return scan: -> bool { true/false }, -> Option<_> { None }, empty bodies
- Hedge-phrase grep: not yet, for now, best effort, no-op

## Findings table

| # | Location | Category | Verdict |
|---|----------|----------|---------|
| 1-29 | mux/src/pane.rs:603-775 + mux/src/tab.rs:4027-5100+ | 29x unimplemented!() in FakePane test doubles | False positive - inside #[cfg(test)] modules (pane.rs:595, tab.rs:4004). Legitimate Rust pattern: test doubles only impl trait methods the tests exercise. |
| 30 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:141 | // TODO: selective_erase when supported (struct-field comment) | Documentation of known terminal-emulator limitation. Not a stub - the struct works without this field. |
| 31 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:1251 | // TODO: see https://vt100.net/docs/vt510-rm/DECSTR.html in Device::SoftReset | Reference comment. The SoftReset handler is fully implemented below - comment is just a spec pointer. |
| 32-33 | term/src/test/c0.rs:13,36 | // TODO: when we can set the left margin, we should test that here | Test-coverage gap. Tests still run; two cases arent covered because the test framework doesnt expose left-margin setup. |
| 34 | term/src/test/mod.rs:9 | // mod selection; FIXME: port to render layer | Historical module disable. The selection test module was moved to the render layer; this comment is a bread-crumb. |
| 35 | mux/src/ssh.rs:957 | fn process_id(&self) -> Option<u32> { None } | Correct by design - WrappedSshChild has no local PID because the child process runs on the remote SSH host. None is the honest answer. |
| 36 | mux/src/ssh.rs:962 | fn as_raw_handle(&self) -> Option<RawHandle> { None } (Windows) | Same rationale as #35. |

## Corrects prior record

The closed bead ft-yv02l (2026-04-20 'Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep') listed the mux tab.rs and pane.rs unimplemented!() clusters under 'Cat 2' and deferred them as 'out-of-scope, pane 1 (mux) territory' requiring domain-owner review. That classification was incorrect - these are test scaffolding inside #[cfg(test)] modules. There is no pane-1-owner action required.

## Outcome

- 0 production stubs
- 0 actionable TODOs (all 5 are documentation / test-coverage / historical disables)
- 0 commits required
- Memory note recorded for future sweeps

## Why this bead exists

Audit trail so future /mock-code-finder passes over the frankenterm/mux|term|codec cluster can skip the scan (its mature WezTerm-heritage code) and correct the residual noise from ft-yv02l #6-7.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:33:07.647071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416510Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416201Z","close_reason":"Verified ft-yv02l #6-7 are #[cfg(test)] FakePane scaffolding only; no production stub to fix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-rfpk6","title":"session_restore + snapshot_engine open_conn helpers omit foreign_keys PRAGMA — cleanup paths leak orphan rows","description":"ft-s4myu fixed StorageHandle::with_config to enable foreign_keys on every connection open. But two OTHER SQLite writers in frankenterm-core have their own open_conn helpers that set journal_mode and busy_timeout but skip foreign_keys:

  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:254 — used by delete_session, load_checkpoint_by_id, list_sessions, show_session, session_doctor, and 5+ other production paths.
  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:1293 — used by cleanup_sync which does 'DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE checkpoint_at < ?1' and 'DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE id IN (...)'. The mux_pane_state → session_checkpoints FK with ON DELETE CASCADE at schema line 646 is supposed to cascade the child deletion. With FKs OFF, cleanup silently leaves orphan mux_pane_state rows.

Fix: add 'PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON' to both open_conn helpers. Regression test: run snapshot_engine::cleanup against a populated DB, assert no orphaned mux_pane_state rows remain.

Scope: same shape as ft-s4myu (compare session_retention.rs:287 which correctly issues the pragma).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:28.078383Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:28.078383Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-riz42","title":"Migrate wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl from direct smol to asupersync","description":"Both crates have direct smol::Async<T>, smol::channel, smol::future::or, and async_io::IoSafe usage that needs asupersync equivalents. Key challenge: smol::Async<T> wraps blocking FDs with reactor - asupersync uses capability-based IoCap model instead. Needs design work for the I/O wrapping layer. Blocked by: understanding asupersync IoCap API and creating an Async<T>-like adapter.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmCreek","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:02:11.906232Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:01.015173Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:01.014992Z","close_reason":"Completed direct Smol removal in wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl; remaining debt tracked in ft-xbnl0.2.1 verification finish-line work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2733,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"TopazHarbor research: asupersync uses IoRegistration (per-type, not generic Async<T>). TcpStream/UnixStream each embed Option<IoRegistration> directly. To build generic Async<T>: wrap T:AsRawFd + IoRegistration + implement AsyncRead/AsyncWrite with rearm pattern. Alternative: add feature-gated conditional compilation (async-smol vs async-asupersync) like SSH crate, keeping smol path until native mux replaces these legacy crates.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:22:30Z"},{"id":2827,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Code-grounded execution split after validating current sources and Asupersync primitives on 2026-04-03.

What the code says now
- `frankenterm/client` and `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl` are the direct Smol entrypoints.
- Support crates already partially know about native Asupersync:
  - `codec` defaults to `async-asupersync` and only stays on Smol when callers force `async-smol`.
  - `frankenterm-ssh` defaults to `async-asupersync` and already exposes a runtime shim (`runtime::block_on`, channel/io reexports).
  - `frankenterm-uds` and `async_ossl` default to `async-asupersync`, but today they only expose blocking `Read`/`Write` wrappers plus optional `async_io::IoSafe`.
- The hard blockers are therefore not abstract runtime folklore; they are concrete call sites and wrapper gaps:
  1. local Unix / mux-server dispatch path
  2. SSH proxy / pipe path
  3. TLS/OpenSSL path

Important design correction
- The first idea in this bead description about creating an Async<T>-like generic adapter should NOT be the default architecture.
- Asupersync's native pattern is per-type `Option<IoRegistration>` plus `rearm(...)` on `WouldBlock`, as seen in its own `net::unix::UnixStream` and `process` pipe wrappers.
- The finish-line-safe approach is: use concrete per-type native adapters where the project owns the type, and only fall back to a narrow local adapter when there is no better owned boundary.

Recommended implementation slicing
1. Local Unix + mux-server dispatch first.
   files: `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, relevant `Cargo.toml` files.
   plan: add native Asupersync `AsyncRead`/`AsyncWrite` + readiness registration to `frankenterm-uds::UnixStream`; stop forcing `codec`/`uds`/`async_ossl` into Smol mode from client and mux-server manifests; replace `smol::Async` + `smol::channel` + `smol::future::or` in dispatch and the local client path with native Asupersync-shaped loops.

2. SSH proxy / pipe path second.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, possibly `frankenterm/ssh/*` only if needed.
   plan: remove direct `smol::block_on` in favor of the SSH runtime shim immediately; then decide whether `SshStream` should gain a small local Asupersync pipe-registration adapter or reuse an existing process-pipe pattern from Asupersync. This path is not the same problem as Unix sockets and should not be conflated with them.

3. TLS/OpenSSL path third, coordinated with finish-line networking work.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
   plan: either give `AsyncSslStream` a native Asupersync readiness wrapper using the same `IoRegistration`/`WouldBlock` pattern, or deliberately hand this slice to the broader native-network/TLS lane if the correct answer is to stop using this wrapper entirely. Do not hide TLS behind another executor-neutral shim.

Immediate low-risk cleanup opportunities once the first slice starts
- direct `smol::block_on(sess.exec(...))` in `client.rs` should route through `frankenterm-ssh`'s runtime shim rather than calling Smol directly.
- `smol::Timer::after(...)` in `client.rs` should move to a native timer path (`asupersync::time` or a repo-local native shim) rather than survive as a stray holdout.

This bead should now be read as: concrete per-transport native cutover, not "invent a new generic Async<T> compatibility layer".","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:04:26Z"},{"id":2831,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Progress update 2026-04-04.

Completed in-code cleanup before the larger stream-wrapper migration:
- `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`
  - `client_thread(...)` now uses `promise::spawn::block_on(...)` instead of importing Smol `block_on`.
  - SSH exec call sites now use `wezterm_ssh::runtime::block_on(...)` instead of `smol::block_on(...)`.
  - version-compat timeout now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.
- `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`
  - title propagation debounce now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.

This does NOT close the bead. Remaining direct Smol/async-io debt in `client.rs` is still the main work:
- `smol::channel::{bounded, unbounded, Receiver, Sender}`
- `smol::Async`
- `smol::future::or`
- `async_io::IoSafe` bounds for the Unix/SSH/TLS stream path

Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail health is green, but stateful reads/writes are currently failing with `DATABASE_ERROR` / `no such table: projects`, so file reservation and inbox checks are temporarily unavailable from this session.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:08:29Z"},{"id":2834,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Implemented the first native Asupersync transport slice in frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs: UnixStream now carries feature-gated async-asupersync AsyncRead/AsyncWrite(+vectored) impls backed by lazy nonblocking conversion plus IoRegistration re-arm, without removing the existing blocking Read/Write surface. Added an async roundtrip regression that specifically exercises an initial WouldBlock before data arrives. Remote cargo check caught a broad test ambiguity from importing AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt at module scope; that is fixed by scoping the imports to the new async test, and a fresh rch check is now in flight. This keeps the local Unix lane moving without inventing a generic Async<T) wrapper and sets up the next step as caller cutover in wezterm-client/local mux dispatch or parallel extension of the same pattern to async_ossl for the TLS lane.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:24:55Z"},{"id":2839,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-04 progress update.

Verified remotely via rch:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p async_ossl --all-targets` passed after fixing the dual-trait vectored write ambiguity in `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client --all-targets` passed after moving `wezterm-client` Unix/TLS transport ownership onto the native wrappers: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs` now boxes raw `UnixStream` and `AsyncSslStream` directly, and `frankenterm/client/Cargo.toml` now requests `wezterm-uds`/`async_ossl` with `async-asupersync` features instead of `async-io`. The SSH proxy path is now the remaining direct `smol::Async` holdout in `client.rs`.

`frankenterm-mux-server-impl` narrowed to one concrete integration issue: `dispatch.rs` erased `wait_for_readable()` behind a non-Send boxed future, which made `crate::dispatch::process(stream)` fail `spawn_into_main_thread` Send bounds from `src/local.rs`. I tightened the dispatch trait to require a Send boxed readiness future and kicked off a fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets`; result still pending as of this comment.

Design status: the transport slice is now materially smaller and clearer. Local Unix and TLS are on the owned Asupersync-backed stream types; the next native cut after this rerun is the SSH split-FD stream and then the smol channel/or loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:58:56Z"},{"id":2840,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"Follow-up verification: the fresh rerun `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets` passed after tightening `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs` so `DispatchStream::wait_for_readable()` returns a Send boxed future. That clears the local Unix/TLS transport slice across `async_ossl`, `wezterm-client`, and `frankenterm-mux-server-impl`; the remaining direct Smol surface in this bead is now concentrated in the SSH proxy stream plus the smol channel/or loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:59:45Z"},{"id":2923,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Taking the next concrete slice of ft-riz42: switching wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl onto codec async-asupersync, replacing their remaining smol channel/or coordination loops, and removing the SSH smol::Async<SshStream> wrapper in favor of a native split-FD readiness wrapper. I’m reading the filedescriptor/asupersync APIs now before patching.","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:09:27Z"},{"id":2925,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Implemented the remaining direct Smol cut in the current ft-riz42 slice. Changes: frankenterm/client and crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl now request codec with async-asupersync; both crates dropped direct smol deps in favor of async-channel plus asupersync; client.rs replaced the smol future/or and channel coordination loop with futures select, replaced the SSH smol Async wrapper with a native split-FD SshStream that implements asupersync AsyncRead and AsyncWrite via IoRegistration, and swapped the version-timeout or-path to select; dispatch.rs replaced smol channel unbounded plus smol future/or with async_channel plus futures select. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets passed after the borrow and lifetime fixes; broad rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets is running now. Workspace cargo fmt --check currently reports unrelated pre-existing diffs outside my edit surface, while rustfmt --check passes on the two edited Rust files.","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-rrrn5","title":"[SEV] CommandBuilder env_map_serde silently collides duplicate keys after to_string_lossy","description":"frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs env_map_serde (added in 803356bf) maps BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry> -> JSON via OsString::to_string_lossy for keys. Two distinct OsString keys whose non-UTF-8 bytes differ but collapse to the same String under lossy conversion will (1) produce a JSON map containing duplicate keys during serialize (most JSON parsers keep last, silently dropping one entry), and (2) on deserialize the BTreeMap::insert loop overwrites on collision, again silently dropping entries without returning an error. The adapter offers no detection or rejection of this case — a corrupted env set roundtrips to a smaller env set with no diagnostic. Metamorphic property: |deserialize(serialize(m))| == |m| — currently violated for any m with keys that collide under to_string_lossy. Conformance concern: the commit message asserts 'POSIX env names are ASCII-only' but setenv(3) on Linux accepts non-ASCII bytes (LC_*=ja_JP.UTF-8 etc.), and libc-level execve passes raw bytes. Fix by either (a) erroring on lossy conversion in both directions, or (b) serializing as an array of [key_bytes, value] pairs via serde_bytes / base64 so the roundtrip is lossless. At minimum add a proptest that asserts roundtrip preserves map size when two synthesized OsString keys share a lossy-equivalent representation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:30.464855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565957Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565787Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7a452b96: replaced map adapter with sequence-of-pairs adapter so every distinct OsString key survives serde roundtrip losslessly (via OsString's native byte/UTF-16-preserving enum encoding in value position). Added 2 regression proptests: one generates arbitrary byte keys (many non-UTF-8) and asserts all distinct keys roundtrip with paired values intact; the other injects two different invalid-UTF-8 sequences that both lossy-render to U+FFFD and asserts both survive. Test-binary run is blocked by pane6-target lock contention; fix is structurally complete and compiles clean at my previous build pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-s9oci","title":"mux/domain follow-ups: blocking spawn_command in async, silent write! errors","description":"Deferred during /multi-pass-bug-hunting audit shipped as dcaf60ca. Three pass-1/2 findings were clean in domain.rs, but two design-level issues remain as follow-up work:

1. Blocking spawn inside async fn. LocalDomain::spawn_pane (domain.rs:667) calls pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd) which on Unix is a synchronous fork+exec. If the child binary or an interposed fork hook hangs, the async task's executor thread is blocked indefinitely. Fix direction: route the spawn through tokio::task::spawn_blocking (or the asupersync equivalent) and await the join handle. Scope: also audit pair.master.take_writer() on the same path.

2. Silently swallowed write! errors. LocalDomain::spawn_pane on the child-spawn-failure branch (domain.rs:693) renders the error into the new pane via write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok(). If the write itself fails the user sees a blank pane with no indication of the upstream spawn failure. Fix direction: log the nested error via tracing::warn! with both the original spawn err and the write err so the failure is at least visible in logs.

Auditor: pane 6 (2026-04-20). Audit context: 91% context budget, committed primary fix + this bead for the residual scope before /clear.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:59:03.628753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409355Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409051Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5c8c417c. Item 1: spawn_command now runs via promise::spawn::spawn_into_new_thread so fork+exec no longer blocks the async executor thread. Item 2: silent write! errors were already fixed earlier in f6019f0f (replaced .ok() with and_then(flush)+tracing::warn! on both err paths).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async-safety","domain","follow-up","mux"]}
 {"id":"ft-sl1xk","title":"shell_escape must not turn empty restore cwd into bare cd","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:31:45.320983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107651Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107317Z","close_reason":"already landed in 3b525792","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-swa8k","title":"[SEV] workflows/runner.rs orphan-cx fallbacks break ambient-cx inheritance in 3 sites","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs lines 729, 1498, 2021 all use the stale fallback pattern:

  let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
  let injector_cx = cx.unwrap_or(&request_cx);

When the caller passes cx=None but an ambient Cx is present (Cx::current() is Some), this falls back to a brand-new for_request() cx instead of inheriting the ambient one. That is the exact bug commit 02a599a9 (refactor(workflows): inherit ambient cx in pane injection wrappers) fixed for the four WorkflowContext::send_* entry points by switching to:

  let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Concrete consequences:
- Line 729 (run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup): set_decision_capture runs under an orphan cx, so a parent cancellation cannot propagate through the capture adapter.
- Line 1498 (run_workflow_inner step loop send_text branch): policy-gated send_text for legacy non-cx callers injects under an orphan cx; parent cancellation does not abort the injection or its audit write.
- Line 2021 (pub async fn run event loop child spawn): runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx is invoked with a fresh cx, orphaning the spawned workflow from the runtime's ambient cx — cancelling the ambient cx does NOT cancel the child workflow.

Real non-test callers hit these paths: mcp_tools.rs:2145 calls runner.run_workflow(...) from an MCP handler (MCP handlers run under an ambient request cx), so MCP-initiated workflows run to completion even if the MCP request is cancelled. The run() event loop at line 1955 is the non-cx sibling of run_with_cx (line 2154) and can be invoked from inside a with_cx block — children spawned there escape the ambient scope.

Fix: replace each 'let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();' with the ambient-inheriting pattern 'let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);'. No behaviour change when no ambient cx is present; closes the ambient-inheritance gap for every caller that already has one.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T22:07:15.266712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792231Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792041Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 3 stale for_request() fallbacks with Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request) in runner.rs (replay_capture setup, step-loop send_text, run() event-loop child spawn). cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes clean. Commit 09014aae.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-szuzd","title":"mcp_tools: schema-declared numeric maxima on wa.cass_* and wa.wait_for/wa.send are not enforced by handlers","description":"Several wa.* MCP tools declare numeric bounds in their input_schema but only enforce the LOWER bound at runtime. The UPPER bound is silently ignored because serde_json doesn't honour JSON-Schema's maximum (that's exactly why ft-tzwuw added the explicit timeout_secs == 0 check). Same shape applies to the maximum side:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:526 wa.cass_search declares 'timeout_secs: {minimum: 1, maximum: 600}' but only checks == 0 at :572. A client sending timeout_secs: 3600 is accepted; cass_client_with_timeout(3600) builds a 1-hour timeout; cass can block the mcp server for up to an hour.
- Same file wa.cass_search declares 'limit: {minimum: 0, maximum: 1000}' with no runtime bound at all — a client sending limit: u64::MAX stages that into CassSearchOptions and the downstream cass call.
- wa.cass_view (:657), wa.cass_expand (:763), wa.wait_for (:1202-area), wa.send (:1926-area) repeat the same shape for timeout_secs.

wa.events at :1725-1749 shows the gold-standard pattern (LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX constants + matching error message). Fix: replicate that pattern for every tool whose schema declares a minimum/maximum that the handler doesn't already enforce. Scope this bead to wa.cass_search first (both bounds); sibling tools follow-on with pointers to this bead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t0gd0","title":"retry: typed RuntimeOperation cancellation remains retryable and trips circuit breaker","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:31.764204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:18.840048Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:18.839178Z","close_reason":"Landed in d2c0dac7 (fix(retry): treat RuntimeOperation::Cancelled the same as Error::Cancelled)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t62hq","title":"[SEV] mcp wa.wait_for + wa.send accept timeout_secs=0 despite schema minimum:1 — silent zero-duration wait","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs WaWaitForTool (line 1085) and WaSendTool (line 1771) advertise input_schema with 'timeout_secs: { type: integer, minimum: 1, default: 30 }' (lines 1095 and 1783). WaitForParams.timeout_secs and SendParams.timeout_secs are both u64 in mcp_types.rs (lines 232, 241), which serde_json::from_value accepts 0 for — serde does not enforce JSON-Schema min/max/default. No post-parse bounds check exists at either call site.

Concrete defect: a client sending { pane_id: 1, pattern: 'OK', timeout_secs: 0 } to wa.wait_for hits line 1166 'let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);' with timeout = 0. The wait loop times out immediately; the tool reports matched: false, polls: 0, elapsed_ms: 0 even if the pattern would have appeared in the pane microseconds later. Same gap in wa.send at line 1928. Clients that type-coerce (e.g., JS Math.floor(0.5) = 0, CLI timeout=0 as 'no timeout') land on this silently.

Precedent: the wa.events tool at line 1638 already enforces its advertised 'limit: minimum:1, maximum:1000' bounds post-parse with a well-documented comment stating 'many MCP clients don't validate tool inputs against the schema before sending'. That comment applies equally to timeout_secs.

Fix: add the same post-parse validation for timeout_secs at both call sites, returning MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a message pointing at the schema's minimum. Pattern matches the wa.events enforcement block exactly.

Also note (tracked separately if filed): wa.cass_search, wa.cass_view, wa.cass_status (lines 511, 621, 711) advertise 'timeout_secs: minimum:1, maximum:600' with the same Rust u64 -> schema mismatch. They pass params.timeout_secs into cass_client_with_timeout (line 278) which accepts any u64. This bead scopes to wa.wait_for + wa.send only to keep the fix focused.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:25:59.494378Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622911Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622531Z","close_reason":"Completed: added post-parse timeout_secs>=1 validation at WaWaitForTool.call and WaSendTool.call matching the wa.events limit-enforcement pattern (line 1638).  {"id":"ft-t995m","title":"escape-parser: tmux_cc feature build broken (130 compile errors) — proptest macro expansion E0277 on Vec<usize>/String strategies","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib --features tmux_cc fails with 130 compile errors. All from the existing proptest block at tmux_cc/mod.rs:1397-1409 (parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant, added by 3abc11d2). Error shape: E0277 'the size for values of type [usize] cannot be known at compilation time' inside proptest::sugar::NamedArguments<N, V> — the macro is trying to use slice-unsized V where it should be Vec-sized. Also 'str' / '[usize]' sized errors with the same root cause. Looks like a proptest 1.11 macro-expansion breakage or a version mismatch between the workspace pin (1.11) and the crate's dev-dep pin (1.8). The tmux_cc feature tests never run in CI when this is broken — my ft-kfy99 fix is latent until this unblocks. Scope: this bead only tracks the test-build breakage; the underlying logic change in ft-kfy99 is unaffected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:05.738611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:05.738611Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
